content_en:
Q. 231. What is the ruling on prayer between the pillars?

A. Praying between the pillars is permissible when there is crowding, but when there is room, one should not pray between the pillars, because it severs the rows. Written on 29/1/

* 1) Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Friday Prayers, in the Chapter: Aman should not make another man stand up from his place then sit in it (911); and by Muslim in the Book of Salutations, in Chapter 11: The Forbiddance of Making a Person Stand up From His Rightful Place Which Was Previously His.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 460 PY vist cabs

Q. 232. What is the ruling on the women’s rows? Is the worst of them the first of them and the best of them the last of them in all circumstances, or when there is no screen between the men and the women?

rer es ps] WE5 By “The best of the women’s rows is the last of them and the worst of them is the first of them.’"} The only reason that this is so, is because the last of them is farthest from the men and the first of them is nearest to the men.

But if there is a private place for them, as there is now in most Masjids, then the best of the women’s rows is the first of them, as is the case with men.

Q. 233. What is the ruling on the prayer of one who prays outside the Masjid, such as one who prays in the road adjacent to the Masjid?

A. If a Masjid is not large enough for the worshippers, and they pray in the roads adjacent to it, then there is no objection, as long as they are able to follow the Imam, because this is necessary. Written on 6/6/1413 A. H.

Q. 234. What is prescribed regarding the straightening of the rows? Is it legislated for the worshipper to connect his ankle to the ankle of the person next to him? Deliver a verdict for us and may you be rewarded.

"I Reported by Muslim in the Book of Prayer, in Chapter 28: Straightening the Rows 1:326:132 (440).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 461 Beall ould

A. The correct opinion is that what is prescribed regarding straightening the rows is for the ankles to be parallel to each other, not the tips of the toes. This is because the body is mounted upon the ankles, while toes differ according to the size of the feet, for there are big feet and small feet, therefore it is not possible to precisely straighten the rows except by the ankles.

As for connecting the ankles, each of them next to the other, there is no doubt that it has been reported from the Companions, may Allah be pleased with them, for they used to straighten the rows by connecting the ankles, each of them next to the other.!"! That is, that each of them would connect his ankle to the ankle of his neighbor, so that they would be parallel and the rows would be straight. And it (i.e. the connecting of the ankles) is not an aim in itself; rather the aim is something other than this (i.e. the straightening of the rows), as the scholars have said. Therefore, if the rows are completed and the people are standing, then it is an obligation on each of them to connect his ankle to the ankle of his companion, in order to achieve straight rows; and the meaning of this is not that he should tenaciously insist on connecting the ankles and keeping them connected throughout the whole of the prayer.

This is a form of excessiveness in this matter as some of the people do, which is that they connect ankle-to-ankle with their companions and spread their feet wide, so that there are spaces between their shoulders and those of their neighbors, thereby contradicting the Sunnah; and the aim is that the ankles be straight.

Q. 235. Is raising the hands in places other than the four (well known) places confirmed? And likewise in the

" 1) There is an indication of this in the words of Anas bin Malik, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: “Each of us would connect his shoulder to the shoulder of his companion and his foot to his foot.” (Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the adhan, in Chapter 76: Connecting the Shoulders (725).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 462 ey! lst Gata

funeral prayer and the ‘Eid prayers?

A. First of all, we must know the four places in which the hands are raised: (They are) upon making the opening Takbir, upon bowing, upon straightening up from bowing and upon standing up from the first Tashahhud. These are the four places whose authenticity has been confirmed by the Hadith from the Messenger of Allah #8, reported on the authority of Ibn ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, who said:

“The Prophet #8 used to raise his hands when he made Takbir for the prayer, when he made Takbir for the Ruku’, when he said: ‘Allah hears the one who praises him.’ And he did not do so in prostration. ay If Ibn ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, who was very careful about following the actions of the Messenger #% — and indeed, he used to follow him - and he saw him raising his hands in Takbir in the Ruku', when straightening up from it and when standing up from the first Tashahhud and he said: “And he did not do so in prostration,” then it may not be said that (raising the hands during prostration) falls under the heading of accepting the affirmative over the negation, and that when a person affirms raising the hands, he is giving precedence to the affirmative over the negation in the Hadith of {bn ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with him. This is because it is clear from the Hadith of bn ‘Umar that he was positive that he did not raise his hands. So, a person who witnessed him raising his hands when he bowed and raising his hands when straightening up from bowing and then says: He did not do that in prostration, can we Say that it is possible that he was inattentive or did not notice? That is not possible, because he was positive that he did

") Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the adhan, in Chapter 83: Raising the Hands in the First Takbir at the Opening of the Prayer (735); and by Muslim in the Book of Prayer, in Chapter 9: The Recommendation of Raising the Hands 21 and 22 (390).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 463 Beall sgh

not do it in prostration and he was positive that he did it when bowing and straightening up from it.

Q. 236. If a person praying behind the Imam caught the prayer while the Imam was bowing, should he make the Takbir twice?

A. Ifa person enters while the Imam is bowing, then he makes the opening Takbir, he should bow immediately and his Takbir for the Ruku ‘ in that case is a Sunnah and not an obligation. If he makes Takbir for the Ruku’, it is better, but if he leaves it, there is no sin upon him.

After that, he will be in one of the following three situations:

1. That he is certain that he reached the Ruku’ before the Imam straightened up from it, in which case, he will have caught the Rak‘ah and he is not required to recite Surat Al-Fatihah in these circumstances.

2. That he is certain that the Imam straightened up from the Ruku’ before he reached it, in which case, he will have missed the Rak‘ah and he must make up for it.

3. That he is uncertain and in doubt as to whether he caught the Imam while he was bowing or whether the Imam had straightened up before he caught him. In this situation, he should base his action on what he thinks is most likely, and if it seems more likely to him that he caught the Jmam in the Ruku‘, then he has caught the Rak‘ah. If it seems more likely to him that he did not catch the Jmam in the Ruku’, then he has missed the Rak‘ah. In this situation, if he has missed something from the prayer, he must perform the prostration of forgetfulness after making the Taslim. If he did not miss anything of the prayer, such as if the Rak‘ah about which he was in doubt was the first Rak‘ah, and he thought it more likely that he had caught it, then in this situation, the prostration of forgetfulness is not necessary, due to his prayer being bound to that of the

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 464 PEY UIST sold

Imam. The Imam takes the responsibility for the prostration of forgetfulness for the one who is being led in prayer, so long as he did not miss anything of the prayer.

There is another situation of doubt which occurs, and this is when a person is uncertain whether he caught the Imam while he was bowing, but he is unable to say which was more likely. In this situation, he should base his action upon what he is certain of, which is that he did not catch the Imam in Ruku’, because that is the basic principle. In which case, he will have missed the Rak‘ah and he must perform the prostration of forgetfulness before the Taslim.

There is still another matter which I would like to bring to your attention in regard to this. Many people, when they enter the Masjid while the Imam is bowing, begin to loudly and repeatedly clear their throats and possibly even speak, saying: ‘Verily, Allah is with the patient ones,’ or they might stamp their feet and all of these things are in contradiction to the Sunnah. It also disturbs the Imam and the worshippers. There are those among the people who, if they enter the Masjid while the Imam is bowing, rush in a most unseemly way. The Prophet # prohibited this, saying:

G5 Egan sles slat J 2b GUY) eas bp 56 8 Gy at

“If you hear the Igamah, then walk to the prayer and do so with

calmness and tranquility, and do not hurry. Then, whatever you catch of the prayer, pray it and whatever you have missed, complete it.’"")

Q. 237. What is the ruling on placing the right hand over the left hand on the chest, or over the heart? What is the ruling on placing the hands below the navel? Is there a difference between a man and a woman?
content_id:
T. 231. Apa hukum salat di antara tiang-tiang?

J. Salat di antara tiang-tiang diperbolehkan ketika terjadi kepadatan, namun ketika masih ada ruang, seseorang tidak boleh salat di antara tiang-tiang, karena hal itu memutus shaf. Ditulis pada 29/1/

* 1) Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Salat Jumat, pada Bab: Seseorang Tidak Boleh Menyuruh Orang Lain Berdiri dari Tempatnya Lalu Duduk di Tempatnya (911); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salam, pada Bab 11: Larangan Menyuruh Seseorang Berdiri dari Tempat yang Menjadi Haknya yang Telah Ditempatinya Terlebih Dahulu.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 460 PY vist cabs

T. 232. Apa hukum mengenai shaf wanita? Apakah yang terburuk adalah yang pertama dan yang terbaik adalah yang terakhir dalam segala keadaan, ataukah ketika tidak ada penghalang antara laki-laki dan perempuan?

rer es ps] WE5 "Sebaik-baik shaf wanita adalah yang terakhir dan seburuk-buruknya adalah yang pertama."} Alasan hal ini demikian tidak lain karena shaf terakhir paling jauh dari laki-laki dan shaf pertama paling dekat dengan laki-laki.

Namun jika ada tempat khusus bagi mereka, sebagaimana yang ada sekarang di kebanyakan masjid, maka sebaik-baik shaf wanita adalah yang pertama, sebagaimana halnya dengan laki-laki.

T. 233. Apa hukum salat seseorang yang salat di luar masjid, seperti orang yang salat di jalan yang berdekatan dengan masjid?

J. Jika masjid tidak cukup luas untuk menampung jamaah, dan mereka salat di jalan-jalan yang berdekatan dengannya, maka tidak ada keberatan, selama mereka mampu mengikuti imam, karena hal ini merupakan kebutuhan. Ditulis pada 6/6/1413 H.

T. 234. Apa yang disyariatkan mengenai meluruskan shaf? Apakah disyariatkan bagi orang yang salat untuk merapatkan mata kakinya dengan mata kaki orang di sebelahnya? Berikanlah fatwa kepada kami dan semoga Anda diberi pahala.

"I Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salat, pada Bab 28: Meluruskan Shaf 1:326:132 (440).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 461 Beall ould

J. Pendapat yang benar adalah bahwa yang disyariatkan mengenai meluruskan shaf adalah agar mata kaki sejajar satu sama lain, bukan ujung jari kaki. Ini karena tubuh bertumpu pada mata kaki, sedangkan jari-jari kaki berbeda menurut ukuran kaki, karena ada kaki yang besar dan ada yang kecil, oleh karena itu tidak mungkin meluruskan shaf secara tepat kecuali dengan mata kaki.

Adapun merapatkan mata kaki, masing-masing bersebelahan dengan yang lain, tidak diragukan bahwa hal itu telah diriwayatkan dari para sahabat, semoga Allah meridhai mereka, karena mereka biasa meluruskan shaf dengan merapatkan mata kaki, masing-masing bersebelahan dengan yang lain.!"! Artinya, masing-masing dari mereka merapatkan mata kakinya dengan mata kaki orang di sebelahnya, sehingga sejajar dan shaf menjadi lurus. Dan hal itu (yaitu merapatkan mata kaki) bukanlah tujuan itu sendiri; melainkan tujuannya adalah sesuatu yang lain (yaitu meluruskan shaf), sebagaimana yang dikatakan para ulama. Oleh karena itu, jika shaf telah lengkap dan orang-orang berdiri, maka wajib bagi masing-masing dari mereka untuk merapatkan mata kakinya dengan mata kaki temannya, demi tercapainya shaf yang lurus; dan makna hal ini bukanlah bahwa ia harus bersikeras merapatkan mata kaki dan menjaganya tetap rapat sepanjang salat.

Ini adalah bentuk berlebihan dalam masalah ini sebagaimana yang dilakukan sebagian orang, yaitu mereka merapatkan mata kaki dengan teman mereka dan merenggangkan kaki lebar-lebar, sehingga ada celah antara bahu mereka dengan bahu orang di sebelahnya, yang justru bertentangan dengan Sunnah; padahal tujuannya adalah agar mata kaki sejajar.

T. 235. Apakah mengangkat tangan di tempat-tempat selain empat tempat yang (masyhur) itu dikonfirmasi? Dan begitu pula dalam

" 1) Terdapat indikasi mengenai hal ini dalam perkataan Anas bin Malik, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang berkata: "Masing-masing kami merapatkan bahunya dengan bahu temannya dan kakinya dengan kakinya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Adzan, pada Bab 76: Merapatkan Bahu (725).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 462 ey! lst Gata

salat jenazah dan salat Ied?

J. Pertama-tama, kita harus mengetahui empat tempat di mana tangan diangkat: (Yaitu) ketika takbiratul ihram, ketika ruku', ketika bangkit dari ruku', dan ketika berdiri dari tasyahhud pertama. Inilah empat tempat yang keabsahannya telah dikonfirmasi oleh hadith dari Rasulullah ﷺ, yang diriwayatkan dari Ibnu 'Umar, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang berkata:

"Nabi ﷺ biasa mengangkat kedua tangannya ketika bertakbir untuk salat, ketika bertakbir untuk ruku', dan ketika mengucapkan: 'Sami'allahu liman hamidah.' Dan beliau tidak melakukannya dalam sujud. ay Jika Ibnu 'Umar, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang sangat berhati-hati dalam mengikuti perbuatan Rasulullah ﷺ — dan sungguh, beliau biasa mengikutinya — melihat beliau mengangkat tangannya saat takbir dalam ruku', saat bangkit darinya, dan saat berdiri dari tasyahhud pertama, lalu ia berkata: "Dan beliau tidak melakukannya dalam sujud," maka tidak boleh dikatakan bahwa (mengangkat tangan saat sujud) termasuk dalam kategori mendahulukan yang menetapkan atas yang menafikan, dan bahwa ketika seseorang menetapkan mengangkat tangan, ia mendahulukan yang menetapkan atas yang menafikan dalam hadith Ibnu 'Umar, semoga Allah meridhainya. Ini karena jelas dari hadith Ibnu 'Umar bahwa ia yakin bahwa beliau tidak mengangkat tangannya. Maka, seseorang yang menyaksikan beliau mengangkat tangannya saat ruku' dan mengangkat tangannya saat bangkit dari ruku' lalu berkata: Beliau tidak melakukannya dalam sujud, dapatkah kita katakan bahwa mungkin ia lalai atau tidak memperhatikan? Hal itu tidak mungkin, karena ia yakin bahwa beliau

") Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Adzan, pada Bab 83: Mengangkat Tangan pada Takbir Pertama saat Membuka Salat (735); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salat, pada Bab 9: Anjuran Mengangkat Tangan 21 dan 22 (390).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 463 Beall sgh

tidak melakukannya dalam sujud dan ia yakin bahwa beliau melakukannya saat ruku' dan bangkit darinya.

T. 236. Jika seseorang yang salat di belakang imam mendapati salat sementara imam sedang ruku', haruskah ia bertakbir dua kali?

J. Jika seseorang masuk sementara imam sedang ruku', maka ia mengucapkan takbiratul ihram, kemudian hendaknya ia segera ruku' dan takbirnya untuk ruku' dalam keadaan tersebut adalah Sunnah dan bukan wajib. Jika ia bertakbir untuk ruku', itu lebih baik, namun jika ia meninggalkannya, tidak ada dosa atasnya.

Setelah itu, ia akan berada dalam salah satu dari tiga keadaan berikut:

1. Bahwa ia yakin telah mendapati ruku' sebelum imam bangkit darinya, dalam hal ini ia telah mendapatkan rakaat tersebut dan ia tidak diwajibkan membaca Surah Al-Fatihah dalam keadaan ini.

2. Bahwa ia yakin imam telah bangkit dari ruku' sebelum ia mendapatinya, dalam hal ini ia telah kehilangan rakaat tersebut dan ia harus menggantinya.

3. Bahwa ia tidak yakin dan ragu apakah ia mendapati imam saat ruku' atau imam telah bangkit sebelum ia mendapatinya. Dalam keadaan ini, ia harus mendasarkan tindakannya pada apa yang ia anggap paling mungkin, dan jika menurutnya lebih mungkin ia mendapati imam dalam ruku', maka ia telah mendapatkan rakaat tersebut. Jika menurutnya lebih mungkin ia tidak mendapati imam dalam ruku', maka ia telah kehilangan rakaat tersebut. Dalam keadaan ini, jika ia kehilangan sesuatu dari salat, ia harus melakukan sujud sahwi setelah salam. Jika ia tidak kehilangan apa pun dari salat, seperti jika rakaat yang ia ragukan adalah rakaat pertama, dan menurutnya lebih mungkin ia telah mendapatkannya, maka dalam keadaan ini, sujud sahwi tidak diperlukan, karena salatnya terikat dengan salat

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 464 PEY UIST sold

imam. Imam menanggung tanggung jawab sujud sahwi bagi makmum, selama makmum tidak kehilangan apa pun dari salat.

Ada situasi keraguan lain yang terjadi, yaitu ketika seseorang tidak yakin apakah ia sempat mendapati imam saat sedang ruku', namun ia tidak dapat menentukan mana yang lebih mungkin. Dalam situasi ini, ia harus mendasarkan tindakannya pada apa yang ia yakini, yaitu bahwa ia tidak mendapati imam dalam ruku', karena itulah prinsip dasarnya. Dalam hal ini, ia dianggap tertinggal satu rakaat dan harus melakukan sujud sahwi sebelum taslim.

Ada perkara lain yang ingin saya sampaikan kepada Anda berkaitan dengan hal ini. Banyak orang, ketika mereka memasuki masjid sementara imam sedang ruku', mulai berdeham dengan keras dan berulang-ulang, bahkan mungkin berbicara dengan mengatakan: 'Sesungguhnya Allah bersama orang-orang yang sabar,' atau mereka menghentak-hentakkan kaki mereka. Semua perbuatan ini bertentangan dengan Sunnah. Hal itu juga mengganggu imam dan para jamaah. Ada pula di antara orang-orang yang, jika mereka memasuki masjid sementara imam sedang ruku', berlari dengan cara yang sangat tidak pantas. Nabi ﷺ melarang hal ini, dengan bersabda:

G5 Egan sles slat J 2b GUY) eas bp 56 8 Gy at

"Jika kalian mendengar iqamah, maka berjalanlah menuju salat dengan

tenang dan penuh ketenangan, dan jangan terburu-buru. Kemudian, apa yang kalian dapati dari salat, kerjakanlah, dan apa yang kalian tertinggal, sempurnakanlah."

T. 237. Apa hukum meletakkan tangan kanan di atas tangan kiri di dada, atau di atas jantung? Apa hukum meletakkan kedua tangan di bawah pusar? Apakah ada perbedaan antara laki-laki dan perempuan?
content_ar: Q. 231. What is the ruling on الصلاة between the pillars? A. Praying between the pillars is مباح when there is crowding, but when there is room, one should not pray between the pillars, because it severs the rows. Written on 29/1/ * 1) Reported by البخاري in the Book of Friday Prayers, in the Chapter: Aman should not make another man stand up from his place then sit in it (911); and by المسلم in the Book of Salutations, in Chapter 11: The Forbiddance of Making a Person Stand up From His Rightful Place Which Was Previously His. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 460 PY vist cabs Q. 232. What is the ruling on the women’s rows? Is the worst of them the first of them and the best of them the last of them in all circumstances, or when there is no screen between the men and the women? rer es ps] WE5 By “The best of the women’s rows is the last of them and the worst of them is the first of them.’"} The only reason that this is so, is because the last of them is farthest from the men and the first of them is nearest to the men. But if there is a private place for them, as there is now in most Masjids, then the best of the women’s rows is the first of them, as is the case with men. Q. 233. What is the ruling on the الصلاة of one who prays outside the المسجد, such as one who prays in the road adjacent to the المسجد? A. If a المسجد is not large enough for the worshippers, and they pray in the roads adjacent to it, then there is no objection, as long as they are able to follow the الإمام, because this is necessary. Written on 6/6/1413 A. H. Q. 234. What is prescribed regarding the straightening of the rows? Is it legislated for the worshipper to connect his ankle to the ankle of the person next to him? Deliver a verdict for us and may you be rewarded. "I Reported by المسلم in the Book of الصلاة, in Chapter 28: Straightening the Rows 1:326:132 (440). الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 461 Beall ould A. The correct opinion is that what is prescribed regarding straightening the rows is for the ankles to be parallel to each other, not the tips of the toes. This is because the body is mounted upon the ankles, while toes differ according to the size of the feet, for there are big feet and small feet, therefore it is not possible to precisely straighten the rows except by the ankles. As for connecting the ankles, each of them next to the other, there is no doubt that it has been reported from الصحابة الكرام رضي الله عنهم, رضي الله عنهم, for they used to straighten the rows by connecting the ankles, each of them next to the other.!"! That is, that each of them would connect his ankle to the ankle of his neighbor, so that they would be parallel and the rows would be straight. And it (i.e. the connecting of the ankles) is not an aim in itself; rather the aim is something other than this (i.e. the straightening of the rows), as the العلماء have said. Therefore, if the rows are completed and the people are standing, then it is an obligation on each of them to connect his ankle to the ankle of his companion, in order to achieve straight rows; and the meaning of this is not that he should tenaciously insist on connecting the ankles and keeping them connected throughout the whole of the الصلاة. This is a form of excessiveness in this matter as some of the people do, which is that they connect ankle-to-ankle with their الصحابة and spread their feet wide, so that there are spaces between their shoulders and those of their neighbors, thereby contradicting السنة النبوية; and the aim is that the ankles be straight. Q. 235. Is raising the hands in places other than the four (well known) places confirmed? And likewise in the " 1) There is an indication of this in the words of Anas bin Malik, رضي الله عنه, who said: “Each of us would connect his shoulder to the shoulder of his companion and his foot to his foot.” (Reported by البخاري in the Book of the adhan, in Chapter 76: Connecting the Shoulders (725). الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 462 ey! lst Gata funeral الصلاة and the ‘Eid prayers? A. First of all, we must know the four places in which the hands are raised: (They are) upon making the opening Takbir, upon bowing, upon straightening up from bowing and upon standing up from the first Tashahhud. These are the four places whose authenticity has been confirmed by الحديث النبوي الشريف from رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم #8, reported on the authority of Ibn ‘Umar, رضي الله عنه, who said: “النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #8 used to raise his hands when he made Takbir for the الصلاة, when he made Takbir for the Ruku’, when he said: ‘الله hears the one who praises him.’ And he did not do so in prostration. ay If Ibn ‘Umar, رضي الله عنه, who was very careful about following the actions of the Messenger #% — and indeed, he used to follow him - and he saw him raising his hands in Takbir in the Ruku', when straightening up from it and when standing up from the first Tashahhud and he said: “And he did not do so in prostration,” then it may not be said that (raising the hands during prostration) falls under the heading of accepting the affirmative over the negation, and that when a person affirms raising the hands, he is giving precedence to the affirmative over the negation in الحديث النبوي الشريف of {bn ‘Umar, رضي الله عنه. This is because it is clear from الحديث النبوي الشريف of bn ‘Umar that he was positive that he did not raise his hands. So, a person who witnessed him raising his hands when he bowed and raising his hands when straightening up from bowing and then says: He did not do that in prostration, can we Say that it is possible that he was inattentive or did not notice? That is not possible, because he was positive that he did ") Reported by البخاري in the Book of the adhan, in Chapter 83: Raising the Hands in the First Takbir at the Opening of the الصلاة (735); and by المسلم in the Book of الصلاة, in Chapter 9: The Recommendation of Raising the Hands 21 and 22 (390). الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 463 Beall sgh not do it in prostration and he was positive that he did it when bowing and straightening up from it. Q. 236. If a person praying behind the الإمام caught the الصلاة while the الإمام was bowing, should he make the Takbir twice? A. Ifa person enters while the الإمام is bowing, then he makes the opening Takbir, he should bow immediately and his Takbir for the Ruku ‘ in that case is a السنة and not an obligation. If he makes Takbir for the Ruku’, it is better, but if he leaves it, there is no sin upon him. After that, he will be in one of the following three situations: 1. That he is certain that he reached the Ruku’ before the الإمام straightened up from it, in which case, he will have caught the Rak‘ah and he is not required to recite Surat Al-Fatihah in these circumstances. 2. That he is certain that the الإمام straightened up from the Ruku’ before he reached it, in which case, he will have missed the Rak‘ah and he must make up for it. 3. That he is uncertain and in doubt as to whether he caught the الإمام while he was bowing or whether the الإمام had straightened up before he caught him. In this situation, he should base his action on what he thinks is most likely, and if it seems more likely to him that he caught the Jmam in the Ruku‘, then he has caught the Rak‘ah. If it seems more likely to him that he did not catch the Jmam in the Ruku’, then he has missed the Rak‘ah. In this situation, if he has missed something from the الصلاة, he must perform the prostration of forgetfulness after making the Taslim. If he did not miss anything of the الصلاة, such as if the Rak‘ah about which he was in doubt was the first Rak‘ah, and he thought it more likely that he had caught it, then in this situation, the prostration of forgetfulness is not necessary, due to his الصلاة being bound to that of the الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 464 PEY UIST sold الإمام. The الإمام takes the responsibility for the prostration of forgetfulness for the one who is being led in الصلاة, so long as he did not miss anything of the الصلاة. There is another situation of doubt which occurs, and this is when a person is uncertain whether he caught the الإمام while he was bowing, but he is unable to say which was more likely. In this situation, he should base his action upon what he is certain of, which is that he did not catch the الإمام in Ruku’, because that is the basic principle. In which case, he will have missed the Rak‘ah and he must perform the prostration of forgetfulness before the Taslim. There is still another matter which I would like to bring to your attention in regard to this. Many people, when they enter the المسجد while the الإمام is bowing, begin to loudly and repeatedly clear their throats and possibly even speak, saying: ‘Verily, الله is with the patient ones,’ or they might stamp their feet and all of these things are in contradiction to السنة النبوية. It also disturbs the الإمام and the worshippers. There are those among the people who, if they enter the المسجد while the الإمام is bowing, rush in a most unseemly way. النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # prohibited this, saying: G5 Egan sles slat J 2b GUY) eas bp 56 8 Gy at “If you hear the Igamah, then walk to the الصلاة and do so with calmness and tranquility, and do not hurry. Then, whatever you catch of the الصلاة, pray it and whatever you have missed, complete it.’"") Q. 237. What is the ruling on placing the right hand over the left hand on the chest, or over the heart? What is the ruling on placing the hands below the navel? Is there a difference between a man and a woman?
